Form title: Petition for Support and/or Allocation of Custodial Responsibility
Function / when used: Starts a stand-alone custody/support case, especially for unmarried parents or non-divorce custody filings.wikipedia+2
Used to modify an existing custody/support arrangement.wikipedia+1

Form title: Petitioner's Civil Case Information Statement Domestic Relations Cases
Function / when used: Required in all case filings according to the judiciary forms list; Legal Aid also says the civil case information sheet is used when filing a custody modification case.wikipedia+1
Financial disclosure form used in custody-related cases, especially where support is involved.wikipedia+2
Sets out the parenting schedule, decision-making, and other custodial terms.wikipedia+1

Form title: Worksheet for Individual Proposed Parenting Plan
Function / when used: Worksheet used to prepare the proposed parenting plan.wikipedia+1
Requests temporary orders on support or custodial responsibility while the case is pending.wikipedia+1

Form title: Bureau for Child Support Enforcement - Application and Income Withholding Form
Function / when used: Used when children are involved and support enforcement or withholding is needed.wikipedia+1
Used if the respondent voluntarily accepts the papers.wikipedia+1

Form title: Affidavit of Out-of-State or Unknown Residency
Function / when used: Used if the respondent’s address is unknown or the respondent lives out of state.wikipedia+1
Used when personal service cannot be completed and publication is needed.wikipedia+1
Used for later filings served on the other side after the initial petition.wikipedia+1
Used when disclosure of address or phone number would risk safety, liberty, or health.wikipedia+1

Form number: not listed in statewide family court table excerpt
Function / when used: Used if the court instructs a party to send notice of hearing to the other side.wvfrn

Form title: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act Waiver: Waiver of Rights to Request Stay of Proceedings
Function / when used: Relevant when active-duty military protections may apply.wikipedia+1
